# Genomic Prediction {#sec-genomic-prediction}

## Internal GBLUP (Known h²)

```{r}
#| eval: false
pop <- breeding.diploid(
  pop,
  bve = TRUE,
  bve.gen = 1:5,
  heritability = 0.5     # Must provide h²
)
```

## rrBLUP Package

```{r}
#| eval: false
pop <- breeding.diploid(
  pop,
  bve = TRUE,
  bve.gen = 1:5,
  bve.rrblup = TRUE     # Uses rrBLUP::mixed.solve
)
```

## Bayesian Methods (BGLR)

```{r}
#| eval: false
pop <- breeding.diploid(
  pop,
  bve = TRUE,
  bve.gen = 1:5,
  bve.bglr = TRUE,
  bglr.model = "BayesB"   # BayesA, BayesB, BayesC, etc.
)
```

## BLUPF90

```{r}
#| eval: false
pop <- breeding.diploid(
  pop,
  bve = TRUE,
  bve.gen = 1:5,
  bve.blupf90 = TRUE,
  blupf90.path = "/path/to/blupf90"
)
```

## Cross-Validation

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Assess prediction accuracy
accuracy <- analyze.bv(
  pop,
  gen = 5,
  cohorts = "TestSet",
  bve.gen = 1:4         # Training set
)

print(accuracy$correlation)  # Accuracy
print(accuracy$bias)          # Bias
```

## Summary
- Multiple BVE methods
- Integration with R packages and external software
- Accuracy assessment

Continue to [Chapter 14: Population Structure](14-population-structure.qmd)!
